channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Ex-central banker to replace Trudeau as Canada's prime minister after winning Liberal Party vote
item.description: Former central banker Mark Carney will become Canada’s next prime minister after the governing Liberal Party elected him its leader as the country deals with U.S. President Donald Trump’s trade war and annexation threat, and a federal election looms.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Noem taps new ICE leadership to bring back accountability and results
item.description: U.S. Department of Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem tapped Todd Lyons and Madison Sheahan to lead U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Air Force intercepts aircraft flying in a restricted zone near Mar-a-Lago
item.description: Air Force fighter jets on Sunday intercepted a civilian aircraft flying in the temporarily restricted airspace near President Donald Trump’s Florida home, bringing the number of violations to more than 20 since Trump took office on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Stacey Abrams slammed after defending $2 billion in Biden-era EPA funds to buy green energy appliances
item.description: Former Democratic Georgia gubernatorial candidate Stacey Abrams is under fire from conservatives after defending a $2 billion initiative under the Biden EPA.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: D.C.'s Black Lives Matter mural will be erased. Look back at the iconic street painting
item.description: Over the past five years, Washington, D.C.'s iconic Black Lives Matter street painting has served as a powerful symbol of activism and a gathering place for joy and resistance.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Trump downplays business concerns about uncertainty from his tariffs and prospect of higher prices
item.description: President Donald Trump is dismissing business concerns over the uncertainty caused by his planned tariffs on a range of American trading partners and the prospect of higher prices.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump DOJ's limits on FACE Act enforcement fuel concern from abortion providers
item.description: New Justice Department leaders say past enforcement of the Freedom of Access to Clinic Entrances Act is "the prototypical example" of what they call "the weaponization of law enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Trump's transactional approach to diplomacy is a driving force on the world stage
item.description: Donald Trump's style of diplomacy is a whole lot like a high-stakes business deal, and his No. 1 goal is to come out of the transaction on top.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump-district Democrats face risky vote on GOP spending bill
item.description: House Democrats in districts carried by President Trump are confronting a potentially risky vote next week when GOP leaders are expected to put their partisan government funding bill on the floor.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: South Dakota law raises questions about future of massive Midwest pipeline
item.description: A new law in South Dakota prohibiting the use of eminent domain to acquire land for carbon capture pipelines challenges the viability of a proposed project snaking through five Midwest states.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Raza And Jack Dura, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China learned from Trump's first trade war and changed its tactics when tariffs came again
item.description: The leaders of both Canada and Mexico got on the phone with President Donald Trump this past week to seek solutions after he slapped tariffs on their countries, but China’s president appears unlikely to make a similar call soon.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: FEMA cancels classes at national fire training academy amid federal funding cuts
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The country's preeminent federal fire training academy canceled classes, effective immediately, on Saturday amid the ongoing flurry of funding freezes and staffing cuts by President Donald Trump's administration.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House GOP seeks spending cuts, defense increases to avert shutdown
item.description: The bill's rollout kicks off a crucial stretch for GOP leadership to lock down support ahead of Friday's shutdown deadline with a Trump-backed strategy that has drawn the ire of Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Tribes and Native American students sue over Bureau of Indian Education firings
item.description: NORMAN, Okla. (AP) — Three tribal nations and five Native American students say in a lawsuit that the Trump administration has failed its legal obligations to tribes when it cut jobs at Bureau of Indian Education schools.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans call for Trump to cut off American Bar Association
item.description: Republican senators signed a scathing letter to the American Bar Association, calling out the organization's alleged political biases against President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congress unveils spending plan after Trump calls on Republicans to avoid government shutdown
item.description: Republicans are unveiling a new plan to fund the government past the coming shutdown deadline next week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ICE raids at schools: Federal judge gives green light
item.description: A federal judge ruled against Denver Public Schools efforts to block immigration officials from carrying out raids on school grounds, marking a win for the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump is forcing a generational shift in GOP foreign policy. Here's how Republicans are responding
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans in Congress have long been intent on countering America's rivals and spreading U.S. influence abroad.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House unleashes on 'rogue bureaucrats' after agency head refuses DOGE entry to headquarters
item.description: The president of the U.S. African Development Foundation filed a lawsuit against the Department of Government Efficiency after the cost-cutting team entered the agency's office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump withdraws slew of Biden-era lawsuits tied to abortion, racial discrimination, financial regs and more
item.description: President Donald Trump has utilized his new powers to end a slew of lawsuits initiated under former President Joe Biden, including challenges to state abortion bans, complaints of racism at police and fire departments and more.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here's what happened during President Donald Trump's 7th week in office
item.description: Trump launched the week telling Americans that his administration would work to take "America’s destiny into our own hands" and vowed that "this will be our greatest era.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ras Baraka has highest favorability among Democratic candidates for NJ governor: poll
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – New polling from Fairleigh Dickinson University gave us some early insight on who voters are looking into in the race for New Jersey governor. Democratic voters weighed in on the candidates' favorability.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Housing policies cited as major reason families forced to give up pets
item.description: Pet rescues and animal shelters hear it over and over: a family has to give up their dog because they had to move and the new place has a rule.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tribal nations are concerned that Trump's cuts have the potential to violate trust responsibilities
item.description: Tribal leaders across the United States are scrambling to respond to a directive from President Donald Trump and Elon Musk to close more than a quarter of Bureau of Indian Affairs offices, which provide vital services to Indigenous communities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk tells Republican lawmakers he's not to blame for federal firings
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s not his fault. Billionaire Elon Musk is telling Republican lawmakers that he is not to blame for the firings of thousands of federal workers, including veterans, as pushes to downsize the government.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump changes course and delays some tariffs on Mexico and Canada
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Thursday postponed 25% tariffs on many imports from Mexico and some imports from Canada for a month amid widespread fears of the economic fallout from a broader trade war.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, JOSH BOAK and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The EU backs a big defense package as Zelenskyy confirms talks with the U.S. next week
item.description: European Union leaders have approved a major spending package on defense, where Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy — a non-EU leader invited to the summit — received a warm welcome.
item.dc:creator: Teri Schultz, MPR News

item.title: Judge declines to immediately spare USAID contractors from firings
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday declined to immediately spare U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) contractors from mass firings, letting move forward a core part of the Trump administration’s effort to dismantle the agency. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: House votes to censure Texas Democrat Al Green for protest during Trump address
item.description: Texas Rep. Al Green, a Democrat, was officially censured Thursday in a vote in the House following his protest on Tuesday during President Trump’s address to a joint session of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Democrats react to Trump's speech; state Republicans applaud his vision
item.description: Democrats who represent Nevada in Washington, D.C., condemned what they see as a failure by President Donald Trump to lower prices for Nevada families, while state Republicans applauded his leadership the morning after his address to a joint session of Congress Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: San Diego Congress member pushes Trump for Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: A San Diego congressional representative is pressing the Norwegian committee who awards the prestigious Nobel Prizes to consider President Donald Trump to be the 2025 recipient of the peace prize.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5
